title: Fleet Robust Decomposition (fleet expand-deep)
description: Adds a robust two-stage goal decomposer for large/ambiguous goals, exposed as a new additive `fleet expand-deep <goal>` sibling subcommand. Its engine runs a bounded coarse PLAN (in-memory outline of named sub-project areas) then an EXPAND of that outline into a children-only FleetManifest validated by the reused validateManifest, all within a fixed total LLM-call budget. Purely additive: the Phase-2 single-shot `fleet expand` path, runFleet, FleetManifestSchema, and the --yes gate stay byte-unchanged.

resolvedClarifications.answer: 2 sprints: Sprint 1 = the robust engine in decomposer-deep.ts (PlanStage, ExpandStage, decomposeGoalDeep, validateOutline, all constants + unit tests, fully testable with a fake LLMClient, no CLI). Sprint 2 = the CLI seam (runFleetExpandDeep + expand-deep subcommand + wiring + byte-lock assertions). Mirrors Phase 2's proven engine-then-CLI split.

Approach A only — build the bounded two-call plan-then-expand engine (DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS=4, no self-judging EXPAND). A shape-valid-but-coarse manifest can pass; it is caught by the unchanged write-and-stop operator review. The bounded critique round (Approach B) stays a future phase, per the architecture's ADR-1.

Shared default output path <root>/.bober/fleet-expand.json — the same default as `fleet expand`. Last-write-wins with an overwrite notice; --out is the escape hatch. This is the architecture's ADR-4 choice.
Approve — write contracts. User explicitly approved the assumptions and out-of-scope sections via the planning gate.

assumptions: The single-shot Phase-2 decomposer decomposeGoal and its constants (DECOMPOSE_SYSTEM_PROMPT, DECOMPOSE_COERCION_INSTRUCTION, DECOMPOSE_MAX_RETRIES) stay byte-unchanged in src/fleet/decomposer.ts:7-187; the robust engine is a NEW sibling file src/fleet/decomposer-deep.ts (evidence: decomposer.ts:159; architecture ADR-2).

validateManifest is already exported from src/fleet/decomposer.ts:95 and is reused verbatim by the EXPAND stage — it inherits JSON-extraction, FleetManifestSchema.safeParse, and the per-child config-key guard (decomposer.ts:95-155). No copy, no fork.

The Outline ({ areas: { name; intent }[] }) is transient and in-memory ONLY — passed directly PlanStage -> ExpandStage within one decomposeGoalDeep call; never serialized, no loader, no schema file, no CLI surface (architecture ADR-3 / Data Model).

The only on-disk artifact remains a FleetManifestSchema-valid children-only manifest ({folder, task}, no config, no concurrency emitted by the engine) at <root>/.bober/fleet-expand.json, written via the reused atomic tmp+rename block (index.ts:206-219).
Both deep LLM calls set jsonObjectMode:true and NEVER set responseSchema — DeepSeek rejects strict json_schema (providers/types.ts:174-183; decomposer.ts:87).

DEEP_PLAN_MAX_RETRIES = 1 and DEEP_EXPAND_MAX_RETRIES = 1, giving a fixed worst-case budget DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS = 4 = (1+1)+(1+1); each stage throws BEFORE any file is written, mirroring decomposeGoal's bounded maxAttempts loop (decomposer.ts:161-186).

runFleetExpandDeep mirrors runFleetExpand (index.ts:169-256) step-for-step and differs ONLY in calling decomposeGoalDeep instead of decomposeGoal; it reuses the same DI seam shape (decompose/runFleet/createClient), credential fail-fast, atomic write, and --yes gate.

registerFleetExpandDeepSubcommand(fleet) is called inside registerFleetCommand IMMEDIATELY AFTER registerFleetExpandSubcommand(fleet) (index.ts:345); it only ADDS .command("expand-deep <goal>") and touches no existing line. The top-level CLI wiring registerFleetCommand(program) (cli/index.ts:317) needs NO change.

Tests use the existing ScriptedClient fake LLMClient pattern (decomposer.test.ts:17-35) that records every ChatParams and the makeFakeDecompose/makeFakeClientBuilder/runFleetSpy DI pattern (expand.test.ts) — zero network in all tests.
--count stays a SOFT target folded into the PLAN prompt (inherited from Phase 2 behavior, index.ts:188-192), not a hard child-count cap.
outOfScope: A self-judging/critique round on the EXPAND output (the bounded generate->critique->revise 'Approach B'); deferred to a future phase per architecture ADR-1.
Any change to the single-shot decomposeGoal / `fleet expand` path, its prompts, or its retry constants (LOCKED byte-identical).
Any change to runFleet, FleetManifestSchema / FleetChildSchema, buildChildConfig, the `fleet <manifest>` command, or its --concurrency/--root options (LOCKED byte-identical).
Persisting the Outline to disk, adding an Outline loader/schema, or any new on-disk contract or CLI surface for the outline.
Introducing a second concurrency model or emitting a concurrency value from the engine (concurrency stays the FleetManifestSchema default of 3, applied at the CLI layer).
A hard --count cap with post-validation truncation (--count remains a soft prompt hint).
A distinct default output path for expand-deep (it shares fleet-expand.json; --out is the escape hatch).
Real DeepSeek network calls in tests, and any change to provider adapters in src/providers/.

features.title: Robust two-stage decomposition engine (decomposer-deep.ts)
features.description: A new sibling module src/fleet/decomposer-deep.ts that orchestrates a bounded PLAN -> EXPAND flow within a fixed total LLM-call budget and returns a validated children-only FleetManifest. PLAN produces a transient in-memory Outline of named sub-project areas via one bounded LLM call; EXPAND turns that Outline into raw text via one bounded LLM call and validates it through the REUSED validateManifest.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: Given a fake LLMClient scripted to return a valid Outline then a valid children-only manifest, decomposeGoalDeep resolves with a FleetManifest that FleetManifestSchema.parse accepts and whose children carry ONLY {folder, task} (no config).

AC2: Given a large/ambiguous goal class on which single-shot validateManifest would yield a degenerate single child, decomposeGoalDeep (PLAN -> EXPAND) returns a multi-child manifest — demonstrated with a scripted client, no network.
AC3: Both client.chat calls captured by the ScriptedClient have jsonObjectMode === true and responseSchema === undefined.

AC4: When every PLAN attempt fails validateOutline, runPlanStage throws after exactly (1 + DEEP_PLAN_MAX_RETRIES) = 2 attempts with an error carrying the accumulated lastError, and ExpandStage is never reached.

AC5: When every EXPAND attempt fails validateManifest (including a child carrying a config key, which the reused config-key guard rejects), runExpandStage throws after exactly (1 + DEEP_EXPAND_MAX_RETRIES) = 2 attempts; total client.chat calls across a fully-failing deep run never exceed DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS = 4.
AC6: validateOutline never throws — it returns { ok:false, error } for non-JSON or shape-invalid outline text, and { ok:true, outline } for a valid { areas: [{name, intent}] } object.
AC7: The coercion retry for each stage uses the 3-message [user, assistant, user] shape (mirroring decomposer.ts:66-81), re-stating the exact required JSON shape in the final user message.
AC8: DEEP_PLAN_MAX_RETRIES === 1, DEEP_EXPAND_MAX_RETRIES === 1, and DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS === 4 (asserted as a fixed audit constant).

features.title: fleet expand-deep CLI subcommand (additive, spawn-safe, byte-lock preserved)
features.description: A new additive `fleet expand-deep <goal>` sibling subcommand and its action runFleetExpandDeep, which mirrors runFleetExpand step-for-step and differs only in calling decomposeGoalDeep. It preserves spawn-safety verbatim (write-before-spawn, write-and-stop default, --yes as the sole spawn gate) and leaves the byte-locked `fleet <manifest>` and `fleet expand` commands untouched.
acceptanceCriteria: AC1: `agent-bober fleet expand-deep <goal>` with no --yes writes a FleetManifestSchema-valid manifest to <root>/.bober/fleet-expand.json (or --out) and does NOT call runFleet (write-and-stop default) — verified with DI stubs (decomposeDeep/runFleet/createClient).
AC2: With --yes, runFleetExpandDeep calls runFleet exactly once with outPath, and the manifest file already exists on disk BEFORE runFleet is invoked (write precedes the spawn gate).

AC3: When createClient throws (missing DEEPSEEK_API_KEY), runFleetExpandDeep rejects with the credential error and writes NO manifest file and never calls decomposeGoalDeep or runFleet (credential fail-fast precedes all IO).
AC4: When decomposeGoalDeep throws (budget exhaustion), runFleetExpandDeep propagates the error, writes NO file, and never calls runFleet.

AC5: registerFleetCommand registers expand-deep as a child subcommand of fleet AND the byte-locked `fleet <manifest>` positional, its --concurrency/--root options, and the existing `expand` subcommand remain intact (asserted on the command tree).
AC6: The expand-deep subcommand exposes the same option set as expand (--count, --provider, --model, --root, --concurrency, --out, --yes).
AC7: --out redirects the write away from the shared default path, and an overwrite of a pre-existing file is atomic (tmp+rename) and logs an overwrite notice.
AC8: `npm run build` and the full `npm test` suite pass; the existing Phase-1/Phase-2 fleet tests (decomposer.test.ts, expand.test.ts, index.test.ts) remain green (no regression).

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: Total LLMClient.chat calls for any robust run are bounded by DEEP_MAX_TOTAL_CALLS = 4; on budget exhaustion the path throws a single clear Error and writes/spawns nothing.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Unit test injects an always-invalid ScriptedClient and asserts the deep run rejects, the recorded calls length <= 4, NO manifest file exists, and runFleet was never invoked.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: No child run may spawn unless the operator could first inspect the manifest: the manifest is always written to outPath before any spawn; write-and-stop is the default; --yes is the sole spawn gate.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: DI test asserts default (no --yes) never calls runFleet, and --yes calls runFleet exactly once AFTER the file exists on disk.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: All robust-mode LLM interaction uses jsonObjectMode:true (loose JSON-object mode), never responseSchema (strict json_schema); provider/SDK types never leak outside src/providers/.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: Assert captured ChatParams on both deep calls has jsonObjectMode===true and responseSchema===undefined; confirm decomposer-deep.ts imports only LLMClient/Message types from providers/types.js.

nonFunctionalRequirements.requirement: The byte-locked surface (runFleet, FleetManifestSchema/FleetChildSchema, the `fleet <manifest>` and `fleet expand` commands, decomposeGoal and its constants) is unchanged.
nonFunctionalRequirements.verificationMethod: git diff shows no edits to those spans; the existing decomposer.test.ts / expand.test.ts / index.test.ts suites stay green; new registration tests assert the locked command tree is intact.

integrationPoints: DeepSeek API via the provider-agnostic LLMClient built by createClient (openai-compat, https://api.deepseek.com), reused from the Phase-2 path (index.ts:178-185).
Reused validateManifest (decomposer.ts:95) consumed verbatim by the EXPAND stage.
Reused atomic tmp+rename write, --yes gate, and runFleet (index.ts:206-256, index.ts:94).
techNotes.dataModel: Transient in-memory only: OutlineArea = { name: string; intent: string }; Outline = { areas: OutlineArea[] }. On-disk: unchanged FleetManifestSchema-valid children-only manifest { rootDir, concurrency, children: [{folder, task}] }.
securityConsiderations: Credential fail-fast: createClient/validateApiKey throws synchronously when DEEPSEEK_API_KEY is missing, BEFORE any FS write or LLM IO.
Spawn-safety: write-before-spawn invariant; write-and-stop default; --yes is the sole gate into runFleet.
Budget bound: both stages throw before the write step, so exhaustion leaves no partial manifest and spawns nothing.
techNotes.existingPatterns: Mirror decomposeGoal's bounded maxAttempts loop and 3-message coercion (decomposer.ts:57-187); mirror runFleetExpand's six steps + DI seam (index.ts:169-256); reuse ScriptedClient (decomposer.test.ts:17-35) and the makeFakeDecompose/makeFakeClientBuilder/runFleetSpy DI helpers (expand.test.ts). ESM .js imports, Zod validation, provider-agnostic boundary, async fs, .bober/ state.

metadata.riskNotes: Additive change against a byte-locked Phase-1/Phase-2 surface. Primary risks: (1) degenerate/coarse children slipping through (mitigated by unchanged write-and-stop review); (2) accidentally using responseSchema instead of jsonObjectMode (DeepSeek rejects — covered by an explicit ChatParams assertion); (3) perturbing the byte-locked command tree (mitigated by appending registerFleetExpandDeepSubcommand AFTER registerFleetExpandSubcommand and asserting the locked tree). The dominant irreversible cost (N detached child runs) is gated behind --yes and unchanged.
